question.text: Once an investor is accredited at the time of onboarding into an AIF scheme, they:

answer.text: Must be re-accredited annually during the scheme's life
Shall be reckoned as an AI through the life of the scheme, even if they lose the status later
Must exit the scheme if their AI status lapses
Must convert to a non-AI investor class

explanation.text: **The Logic:** If an investor is an Accredited Investor at the time of onboarding into an AIF scheme, they shall be reckoned as an AI through the life of the scheme, even if they lose such status in the interim. This provides certainty and prevents disruptions. **The Trap:** This grandfathering applies to AI status within a specific scheme. If the investor wants to invest in a new scheme, they would need current AI status. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is a recent clarification by SEBI and is tested in updated XIX-A curriculum. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This rule provides comfort to investors — they won't be forced out of an AI-only scheme if their financial situation changes. Use this to reassure clients concerned about long-term eligibility.
